\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Free‑space (homogeneous) Green\textquotesingle{}s function provider. 

Implements\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Scalar Green\textquotesingle{}s function $G(r,r\textnormal{\textquotesingle}) = e^{ik|r-r\textnormal{\textquotesingle}|}/(4\pi|r-r\textnormal{\textquotesingle}|)$.
\item Gradient $\nabla^{\prime} G$ and full {\bfseries{dyadic}} Green\textquotesingle{}s function.
\item Smoothed kernels used for singularity subtraction when triangles are close or coincident.
\item Batched assembly helpers over groups of RWG functions living on the same triangles to reduce repeated kernel evaluations. 
\end{DoxyItemize}

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{GreenFHom3D_8h_source_l00035}{35}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{GreenFHom3D_8h_source}{Green\+FHom3\+D.\+h}}.

Computes $\int_{T\textnormal{\textquotesingle}} [ f_{p}(r\textnormal{\textquotesingle}) \; G(r,r\textnormal{\textquotesingle}) - \frac{\nabla\textnormal{\textquotesingle} G(r,r\textnormal{\textquotesingle})}{k^2}\,\mathrm{div} f_p ] \; dS\textnormal{\textquotesingle} $, choosing quadrature and (smoothed vs exact) kernels based on distance heuristics. When near-\/singular, applies analytical singularity subtraction via {\ttfamily \doxylink{classSingSub}{Sing\+Sub}}.

Compute $\mathbf D$ and $\mathbf K$ for all RWG pairs on two triangles. 


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em fvec} & RWGs living on the observation triangle. \\
\hline
{\em fpvec} & RWGs living on the source triangle. \\
\hline
{\em DK} & Output matrix of pairs $(D,K)$ indexed by {\ttfamily }\mbox{[}i\mbox{]}\mbox{[}j\mbox{]}.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
Adapts quadrature and analytic corrections depending on triangle adjacency\+: coincident, edge‑adjacent, vertex‑adjacent, or disjoint.

This routine reuses geometry, quadrature points and kernel evaluations to fill many matrix entries at once\+: {\ttfamily fvec} lives on triangle T, {\ttfamily fpvec} on triangle T\textquotesingle{}. The logic mirrors the single-\/pair routines but amortizes the cost across pairs.

\mbox{\hyperlink{globals_8h_a52196b4b274b585226e0614490f7defc}{dcmplx}} Green\+FHom3\+D\+::\+Smoothed (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{globals_8h_ae4271193b04e5d22237184d4fad54fb0}{rvec}}}]{r}{, }\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{globals_8h_ae4271193b04e5d22237184d4fad54fb0}{rvec}}}]{rp}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})\hspace{0.3cm}{\ttfamily [virtual]}}



Smoothed scalar kernel used under singularity subtraction. 


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em r} & Observation point. \\
\hline
{\em rp} & Source point. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
Complex scalar smoothed kernel.
\end{DoxyReturn}
For small $|r-r\textnormal{\textquotesingle}|$, we add/subtract a local expansion to tame the $1/|r-r\textnormal{\textquotesingle}|$ singularity during area integrations. This returns $G(r,r\textnormal{\textquotesingle}) - 1/(4\pi|r-r\textnormal{\textquotesingle}|) + k^2 |r-r\textnormal{\textquotesingle}| / (8\pi)$ which is bounded at coalescence and integrates well over triangles.
